title: Slope in a table
description: Practice finding the slope for a linear relationship in a table of values.

assessment_items.question_content:
**What is the slope of the line that contains these points?**

$x$ | $5$ | $6$ | $7$ | $8$
-: | -: | -: | -: | -:
$y$ | $-3$ | $-1$ | $1$ | $3$

slope: [[☃ numeric-input 1]]
hints:
$\text{Slope}=\dfrac{\text{Rise}}{\text{Run}}=\dfrac{\text{Change in }y}{\text{Change in }x}$

We can calculate the change in $x$ and change in $y$ by picking any two pairs of corresponding $x$- and $y$-values.

$\begin{array}{lrrrr}
&&\xrightarrow{+1}\,\,\,\,
&\xrightarrow{+1}\,\,\,\,
&\xrightarrow{+1}\,\,\,\,
\\
x
&5
&6
&7
&8
\\
\hline
\\
y
&-3
&-1
&1
&3
\\
&&\xrightarrow[+2]{}\,\,\,\,
&\xrightarrow[+2]{}\,\,\,\,
&\xrightarrow[+2]{}\,\,\,\,
\end{array}$

So the slope is:

$\dfrac{\text{Change in }y}{\text{Change in }x}=\dfrac{2}{1}=2$
The slope is $2$.

**What is the slope of the line that contains these points?**

$x$ | $-4$ | $-3$ | $-2$ | $-1$
-: | -: | -: | -: | -:
$y$ | $2$ | $5$ | $8$ | $11$

slope: [[☃ numeric-input 1]]
hints:
$\text{Slope}=\dfrac{\text{Rise}}{\text{Run}}=\dfrac{\text{Change in }y}{\text{Change in }x}$

We can calculate the change in $x$ and change in $y$ by picking any two pairs of corresponding $x$- and $y$-values.

$\begin{array}{lrrrr}
&&\xrightarrow{+1}\,\,\,\,
&\xrightarrow{+1}\,\,\,\,
&\xrightarrow{+1}\,\,\,\,
\\
x
&-4
&-3
&-2
&-1
\\
\hline
\\
y
&2
&5
&8
&11
\\
&&\xrightarrow[+3]{}\,\,\,\,
&\xrightarrow[+3]{}\,\,\,\,
&\xrightarrow[+3]{}\,\,\,\,
\end{array}$

So the slope is:

$\dfrac{\text{Change in }y}{\text{Change in }x}=\dfrac{3}{1}=3$
The slope is $3$.
